content.full_text:
Assembly
California Legislature

TRICIA HUNTER
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, SEVENTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

P.O. Box 2
Escondido, California 92025
(619) 739-9158

April 12, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Sacramento, California 95814

To Whom It May Concern:

My office administrative assistant, Anthony Russo, has accepted a position with the California Dental Association. He will serve as assistant director of government relations and director of the political action committee. In those positions, Anthony will lobby in the State Assembly and execute campaign decisions made by the association's membership.

I am writing to ascertain if any conflict of interest problems would exist should I pursue one or more of the following scenarios.

(1) Can Mr. Russo provide management advice and/or supervision to my district office staff during an interim period of transition to a new administrative assistant, and can I pay him a stipend from my campaign account for those services?

(2) Can Mr. Russo periodically provide management consulting to my district and/or capitol office staff i.e., writing a general office plan, conducting staff reviews, and/or preparing staff retreats, and can I pay him a stipend from my campaign account for those services?

(3) Can Mr. Russo periodically provide political consulting services to my campaign committee if limited one or more of the following: general campaign management, record-keeping, absentee ballots, telecommunications, and/or direct mail, and can I pay him a stipend from my campaign account for those services?

Thank you for your attention to these questions. Please feel free to contact me at 445-8211 should you have any questions.

Sincerely,

[signature]
TRICIA HUNTER
Assemblywoman
